pages.raw_text:
1t is now up to the prosecuting
attorneys Office, as the legal ad-
visers for the county, to determine
whether or not the county commits
sioners have the right to insist on
the union label for county printing.


The injunction suit brought
against the commissioners by the
nonunion printing Offices was to-
day referred to the prosecutors
Office for an opinion. On his advice
the commissioners will act for or
against the label. One member of
the board is of the opinion that the
action of the commissioners on the
union label can not be sustained
by law.


This question has never been
passed upon by the supreme court
of Washington. The findings in
other states have differed, but the
reponderance of court opinions
seem to lean against the insistence
of the union label on public print
ing, according to attorneys.
